Metric Description: The ratio of CRE loans to the bank's total capital. It assesses the exposure of the bank to the commercial real estate market.
The ratio of a bank's total loans to its total deposits. It indicates the bank's liquidity position and its reliance on external borrowing.
The difference between the interest income generated by the bank and the amount of interest paid out to lenders, relative to the amount of their interest-earning assets.
The ratio of operating expenses to operating income. It evaluates the efficiency of the bank's management.
The ratio of net income to total assets. It measures how efficiently a bank is using its assets to generate earnings.
